title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Sedgwick discusses his desire to sell his land at a high price to secure a tax-avoiding asset, a display of greed that surprises Lucy. During their conversation, Sedgwick mentions the postponed inquest into Alfred's death, which involved a group poisoning incident, and asks about Alexander, who is expected to begin school at Stottard soon.

Lucy calls Miss Maple, who explains she has written to Mrs. McGillicuddy to request her immediate return. When Lucy suggests the possibility of a mentally ill perpetrator, Miss Maple expresses skepticism. Later, while Lucy is eating in the study, Brian arrives and reveals his intention to stay at the mansion because he is unemployed. Brian discusses the arsenic poisoning, noting that the details have not been publicly reported. He expresses a desire for the house to eventually belong to Alexander, remarking that for this to happen, others would have to die first. He also observes that Alexander does not seem particularly saddened by Alfred's death.

In the kitchen, Mrs. Kidd shares scandalous rumors circulating about the family. These include claims that a woman found in the long warehouse was Edmund's mistress, killed by a suspicious husband, and rumors that Harold killed a woman in Rutherford to hide his marriage to Alice. As Lucy processes these rumors, a doorbell rings. Instead of the doctor, a beautiful, elegant woman in a fur coat arrives in a Rolls-Royce, requesting to see Miss Emma.
